summary: Merged decomp-matching sweep across 24 files, dominated by Kirby copied-special codegen fixes plus smaller game-mode, stage, item, trophy, and baselib cleanups. The decomp.dev bot reported 55.12% matched code overall, +0.32% and +12392 bytes, with 52 new matches and one additional grFourside improvement.
what_changed: Kirby Special-N-family code received many small matching fixes: PAD_STACK additions/removals/resizes, local variable reordering, direct constants/enums, and small expression changes in ftKb_SpecialN.c, ftKb_SpecialNNs.c, ftKb_SpecialNPk.c, ftKb_SpecialNYs.c, and ftKb_SpecialNZd.c.
ftKb_Init changed fp->fv.kb.x88 initialization from da->specialn_kp_lowest_charge_graphic_size to da->specialn_kp_flame_scale.

ftKb_SpecialN.c changed several Kirby logic/codegen details, including specialhi xC check to == 1, ftKirbyDmgInline to ftKirbyDmgInline2, hat ability-loss flag access from fp->fv.kb.xF4_b0 to fp->fv.kb.hat.x8_b0, EatTurn motion-state speed from 0.0f to 1.0f, and PAD_STACK sizing around captured/copy ability functions.

ftKb_SpecialNNs.c matched DK copied-neutral-special animation functions by clearing pre_hitlag_cb/post_hitlag_cb through a scoped Fighter* fp2 = gobj->user_data reload and by tuning stack padding, including u8 _[32] in ftKb_DkSpecialNFull_Anim.
gm/types.h replaced TmData pad_x524 with HSD_Text* x524[4], and gm_18A5.c then used td->x524[2] and td->x524[3] directly instead of casting td+0x524 to HSD_Text**.
gm_1BA8 changed gm_801BEFA4 from s8 ckind to int ckind in both implementation and header.
ftchangeparam.c changed ftCo_800D105C so fp->x2C4.x/y are multiplied by a local scale_y = fp->x34_scale.y rather than temp_f1.

Stage files received small codegen fixes: grFourside/grVenom added PAD_STACK(8), grIceMt changed an UnkFlagStruct bitfield write from b7 to b0 and simplified a user_data assignment, and grKongo rewrote a branch to subtract unk52 before testing and asserting.

Item/toy/baselib fixes included it_802E5F8C passing item_gobj instead of NULL as the first it_802E614C argument, un_80312834 returning char* with header update, TyListState::x2A1 changing from u8 to s8, toy.c shrinking a stack buffer from 80 to 72 bytes, hsd_3AA7.c reassociating extra_size = base_size + extra_size, and sislib.c using a chained assignment from HSD_MemAlloc.
smart_moves: Converted a raw offset cast in gm_18A5 into a typed TmData::x524[4] field, improving both matching and future struct readability.
Reused a scoped gobj->user_data reload before callback stores across multiple DK copied-special functions, a targeted way to influence MWCC codegen without changing behavior.
Treated PAD_STACK as a per-function matching knob: some pads were added, some removed, and some resized instead of blindly padding every near-match.
Replaced magic or awkward temporaries with named enum/field expressions where evidence allowed, such as ftKb_MS_FeSpecialAirNLoop and fp->fv.kb.hat.x8_b0.
Kept prototype/header changes synchronized with implementation changes for gm_801BEFA4 and un_80312834.
decomp_lessons: When an offset’s type becomes clear, promote padding to a typed struct field and update callers; this can eliminate pointer arithmetic while preserving matching.
Small source-level expression choices matter for MWCC output: chained assignments, reassociation order, scoped locals, and reloads from gobj->user_data can be decisive.
Stack matching is not monotonic; deleting a dummy local or shrinking PAD_STACK can be as useful as adding padding.
Signedness and return-type corrections in headers can unlock matches outside the immediate function body, as shown by TyListState::x2A1 and un_80312834.
For fighter special-move code, verify copied-ability state fields carefully before naming or moving them; this PR corrected accesses such as hat.x8_b0 and specialn_kp_flame_scale.
naming_conventions: Kirby fighter code uses ftKb_* function prefixes and copied-ability abbreviations such as Dk, Kp, Ca, Ms, Fe, Sk, Mt, Pp, Ss, Fx, and Ns in function and motion-state names.
Offset-derived struct fields remain xNNN-style until semantic names are known, exemplified by TmData::x524 and TyListState::x2A1.
Header prototypes should mirror implementation types exactly: gm_801BEFA4(int ckind) and char* un_80312834(char* buf, u32 num).
When a bitfield’s owner substructure is known, prefer the more specific path, as in fp->fv.kb.hat.x8_b0 rather than a top-level fp->fv.kb.xF4_b0.
Named motion-state constants are preferred over raw numeric IDs when available, as in ftKb_MS_FeSpecialAirNLoop replacing 0x217.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: PAD_STACK values were tuned at several granularities, including 8, 16, 24, and 4 * 12 bytes, and some obsolete dummy pads were removed.
ftKb_SpecialN_800F5D04 matched by ordering Vec3 vel before Vec3 pos and adding PAD_STACK(8).
Multiple DK copied-special functions forced callback-store codegen by using a scoped Fighter* fp2 = gobj->user_data before setting pre_hitlag_cb and post_hitlag_cb to NULL.
sislib.c matched HSD_SisLib_803A6048 by using chained global assignment: HSD_SisLib_804D796C = HSD_SisLib_804D7970 = HSD_MemAlloc(...).
gm_18A5.c matched through typed array access td->x524[n] plus PAD_STACK(24) instead of an HSD_Text** cast from td+0x524.
Other micro-tactics included expression reassociation in hsd_803AC340, explicit cast (s32)(*charge * 30), direct fp->cmd_vars[3] indexing, and nested branch restructuring in grKongo_801D8314.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review approvals were present in the slice.
The only PR comment was the decomp.dev bot report showing +12392 matched bytes, 52 new matches, and one grFourside_801F2F34 improvement.
Merged by ribbanya shortly after the bot report; no recorded requested changes in the available context.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type TmData::x524 or nearby x518/x534 text arrays.
Search for subsequent fixes to Kirby fp->fv.kb.x88, specialn_kp_flame_scale, and copied-ability hat bitfields.
Look for other DK copied-special functions that need the scoped gobj->user_data callback-clearing pattern.
Audit nearby Kirby SpecialN PAD_STACK values to see whether later PRs removed temporary padding after better struct/local typing.
Investigate the 22 omitted decomp.dev new matches not listed in the truncated bot table if exact per-function match attribution is needed.
